title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: vignette shows use NFert 0.6.0 compute full nitrogen, phosphorus potassium fertilisation plan following Disciplinari di Produzione Integrata (DPI) Emilia-Romagna 2026. package reproduces logic official regional tool Fert_Office v1.26 (Febbraio 2026, Regione Emilia-Romagna) makes scriptable R. package implements two complementary methods: Metodo del bilancio (Allegato 2): full nutrient mass balance crop demand, soil supply, losses, previous-crop effects organic contributions. Recommended detailed soil climate data available. Scheda dose Standard: simplified per-crop dose user-selectable decrement increment factors, capped MAS (Massimali Ammessi di Simulazione). Recommended soil analyses limited. package also provides fertilisation distribution plan end--cycle soil P K estimation close agronomic loop planning recording.

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026 — Norme Generali, Allegato 2 (metodo del bilancio), Allegato 9 (massimali), Guida alla Fertilizzazione Minerale e Organica (N, P, K). Reg. reg. 2/2024 — limiti MAS. Reg. reg. 3/2017 — utilizzazione agronomica degli effluenti e del digestato. Direttiva Nitrati 91/676/CEE — ZVN limite di 170 kg N/ha/anno da effluenti zootecnici. Reg. UE 2021/2115 — soglie minime di efficienza.

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: NFert mixes English Italian terminology design: Function names: English snake_case (e.g. N_balance, P_balance, plan_distribution, classify_pH). Three exceptions retain Italian DPI canonical name: scheda_N, scheda_PK, get_MAS / check_MAS. Parameters: English snake_case throughout. Class strings (e.g. “molto bassa”, “media”, “Sabbiosi”) kept Italian DPI canonical labels. Soil-group labels accepted three conventions: Italian plural (“Sabbiosi”, “Medio impasto”, “Argillosi e limosi”), Italian singular (“Sabbioso”, “Franco”, “Argilloso”) English (“Sandy textures”, “Loamy textures”, “Clay textures”). normalise_soil_group() helper canonicalises .
code: library(NFert) normalise_soil_group("Loamy textures") #> $id_rag #> [1] 2 #>  #> $it_plural #> [1] "Medio impasto" #>  #> $it_singular #> [1] "Franco" #>  #> $en #> [1] "Loamy textures" normalise_soil_group("Franco") #> $id_rag #> [1] 2 #>  #> $it_plural #> [1] "Medio impasto" #>  #> $it_singular #> [1] "Franco" #>  #> $en #> [1] "Loamy textures" resolve_id_rag(clay = 18.5, sand = 15.5) #> [1] 2

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: NFert classifies soil along DPI 2026 dimensions. 3-class texture grouping (Sabbiosi / Medio impasto / Argillosi e limosi) drives every subsequent coefficient choice (B, C, D, efficiency, soil weight, etc.).
code: # USDA class + DPI 3-group soil_props <- calc_soil_group_and_id_rag(clay = clay, sand = sand) soil_props #> $soil.group #> [1] "Loamy textures" #>  #> $id_rag #> [1] 2 #>  #> $TRI3 #> [1] "FL"  # pH, calcare, SO, CSC classify_pH(pH) #> $ID_pH #> [1] 6 #>  #> $class #> [1] "alkaline" #>  #> $pH #> [1] 8.3 classify_carbonate_tot(caco3_tot) #> $ID #> [1] 3 #>  #> $class #> [1] "Moderately calcareous" #>  #> $caco3_tot #> [1] 15 classify_carbonate_att(caco3_att) #> $ID #> [1] 3 #>  #> $class #> [1] "High" #>  #> $caco3_att #> [1] 6.8 som_cls <- classify_SOM(SOM = SOM, soil_group = "Medio impasto") som_cls #> $rating #> [1] "medium" #>  #> $class #> [1] "Normal" #>  #> $class_it #> [1] "Normale" #>  #> $SOM #> [1] 2 #>  #> $soil_group #> [1] "Loamy textures"  # Maximum annual SO input (for ammendanti) max_SO_input(som_cls$class) #> [1] 11

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: DPI 2026 formula : Nfert=−B+C1+C2+D−E−F−Forg−G N_{fert} = - B + C_1 + C_2 + D - E - F - F_{org} - G term computed dedicated function:

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: result matches Fert_Office Bilancio sheet: = 186.6, B = 73.84, D = 39.536 (includes |E| = 10), G = 10.05, N required ≈ 142.25 kg/ha.
code: n_bal <- N_balance(   expected_yield_tons_ha = yield,   crop = crop,   ccp  = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days",   sand = sand, clay = clay,   Ntot = Ntot, SOM = SOM, CN = CN,   oxygen_availability = "Normal",   winter_rain = winter_rain,   start_spring_rain = start_spring_rain,   prev_crop = prev_crop,   source = "None",   fertorg_frequency = "every year",   location = location,   forg_quantity = 0,   soil_seeding = soil_seeding,   greenhouse = greenhouse,   E_to_D = TRUE        # fold negative E into D, as in foglio Bilancio )  n_bal #>       A     B b1    b2 C1 C2     D E F Forg    G surplus_pluviometrico #> 1 186.6 73.84 39 34.84 30  0 28.46 0 0    0 13.4                 FALSE  # Final dose n_dose <- calculate_N_fertilization(n_bal) n_dose #> [1] 157.82 # Signed terms in N_fert = A - B + C1 + C2 + D - E - F - Forg - G (kg N/ha) signed <- c(   A = n_bal$A, B = -n_bal$B, C1 = n_bal$C1, C2 = n_bal$C2, D = n_bal$D,   E = -n_bal$E, F = -n_bal$F, Forg = -n_bal$Forg, G = -n_bal$G ) op <- par(mar = c(7, 4, 3, 1)) cols <- grDevices::hcl.colors(length(signed), "BluYl", alpha = 0.9) barplot(   as.numeric(signed), names.arg = names(signed), las = 2, col = cols,   border = NA, ylab = "kg N/ha",   main = "N balance terms (signed toward mineral N need)" ) abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray40") par(op)

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: phosphorus balance follows DPI Allegato 2 logic coded foglio Gri_P: P2O5=Aasp+B1,arric+A2−B2,rid P_2O_5 = A_{asp} + B_{1,arric} + A_2 - B_{2,rid} strategia depending soil Olsen class: Molto bassa / bassa: Arricchimento — add B1=(22.9−P2O5ppm)×w30cm/1000×fimmB_1 = (22.9 - P_2O_5^{ppm}) \times w_{30cm}/1000 \times f_{imm} fimm=1.6f_{imm}=1.6. Media / elevata: Mantenimento — apply = asportazione. Molto elevata: Riduzione — = 0.

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: potassium balance extends formula leaching term H depends clay content: K2O=Aasp+H+B1,arric+A2−B2,rid K_2O = A_{asp} + H + B_{1,arric} + A_2 - B_{2,rid} Classes K defined per DPI texture group (Sabbiosi, Medio impasto, Argillosi e limosi).

code: k_cls <- classify_K(value = K, unit = "K2O", soil_group = "Medio impasto") k_cls #> $value_ppm_K #> [1] 124.9995 #>  #> $value_ppm_K2O #> [1] 150 #>  #> $ID_Gri_K #> [1] 3 #>  #> $rating #> [1] "medium" #>  #> $strategy #> [1] "Mantenimento"  # Leaching by clay (step function): sapply(c(2, 10, 18.5, 30), K_leaching_by_clay) #> [1] 60 30 20 10 clay_seq <- seq(0, 45, length.out = 300) h_seq <- vapply(clay_seq, K_leaching_by_clay, numeric(1)) plot(   clay_seq, h_seq, type = "s", lwd = 2,   col = grDevices::hcl.colors(1, "Teal"),   xlab = "Clay (%)",   ylab = expression("H — K leaching coefficient (kg K"[2]*"O/ha)"),   main = "K balance: clay-dependent leaching term (DPI step function)" ) rug(c(2, 10, 18.5, 30), col = "gray55") points(   c(2, 10, 18.5, 30), sapply(c(2, 10, 18.5, 30), K_leaching_by_clay),   pch = 21, bg = "coral", col = "white", cex = 1.2 )

text: N standard dose modified five decrements five increments, capped MAS. cumulated dose overshoots max_N_dose, function caps flags mas_exceeded = TRUE:
code: # Durum wheat with buried straw + compacted / no-till scheda_N(   crop = crop,   increments = c(straw_burial = 30, compacted_no_till = 10) ) #> Crop 'Durum wheat (whole plant)' has 2 matching rows; using the first. #> $crop #> [1] "Durum wheat (whole plant)" #>  #> $phase #> [1] "nd" #>  #> $dose_base #> [1] 160 #>  #> $total_decrement #> [1] 0 #>  #> $total_increment #> [1] 40 #>  #> $dose_recalculated #> [1] 200 #>  #> $max_N_dose #> [1] 200 #>  #> $dose_final #> [1] 200 #>  #> $mas_exceeded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $units #> [1] "kg N/ha" #>  #> $reference #> [1] "DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026, Fert_Office v1.26 (Scheda_N)"  # Alfalfa after, yield low scheda_N(   crop = crop,   decrements = c(after_alfalfa_meadow = 60, yield_low = 20),   increments = c(low_SOM = 20) ) #> Crop 'Durum wheat (whole plant)' has 2 matching rows; using the first. #> $crop #> [1] "Durum wheat (whole plant)" #>  #> $phase #> [1] "nd" #>  #> $dose_base #> [1] 160 #>  #> $total_decrement #> [1] 80 #>  #> $total_increment #> [1] 20 #>  #> $dose_recalculated #> [1] 100 #>  #> $max_N_dose #> [1] 200 #>  #> $dose_final #> [1] 100 #>  #> $mas_exceeded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $units #> [1] "kg N/ha" #>  #> $reference #> [1] "DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026, Fert_Office v1.26 (Scheda_N)" scheda_N(   crop = crop,   increments = c(straw_burial = 100, compacted_no_till = 100, low_SOM = 50) ) #> Crop 'Durum wheat (whole plant)' has 2 matching rows; using the first. #> $crop #> [1] "Durum wheat (whole plant)" #>  #> $phase #> [1] "nd" #>  #> $dose_base #> [1] 160 #>  #> $total_decrement #> [1] 0 #>  #> $total_increment #> [1] 250 #>  #> $dose_recalculated #> [1] 410 #>  #> $max_N_dose #> [1] 200 #>  #> $dose_final #> [1] 200 #>  #> $mas_exceeded #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $units #> [1] "kg N/ha" #>  #> $reference #> [1] "DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026, Fert_Office v1.26 (Scheda_N)"

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: required N, P, K doses known, plan distributes across epochs methods. plan_distribution() accepts: list organic applications (matrix name organic_fertilizers.table, quantity t/ha, year, modality-epoch id, optional efficiency level). list mineral applications (name mineral_fertilizers.table, quantity q/ha, modality-epoch). Optional target doses (Eccesso/Deficit alerts) ZVN flag.
code: plan <- plan_distribution(   soil_group = "Medio impasto",   n_balance  = n_dose,   p_balance  = p_bal$P2O5_required,   k_balance  = k_bal$K2O_required,   organic_rows = list(     list(fertilizer    = "letame bovino",          quantity_t_ha = 20,          year          = 2025,          modality_epoch = 1,          level         = "media")   ),   mineral_rows = list(     list(fertilizer       = "UREA AGRICOLA PRIL.46%",          quantity_q_ha = 3,          modality_epoch = 11)   ),   zvn = TRUE )  # Row-by-row plan plan$rows #>    source             fertilizer year modality_epoch ID_Mo quantity_t_ha N_kg #> 1 organic          letame bovino 2025              1     1          20.0  100 #> 2 mineral UREA AGRICOLA PRIL.46%   NA             11    11           0.3  138 #>   P2O5_kg K2O_kg efficiency_pct N_useful P2O5_useful K2O_useful zootec #> 1      48    140             50       50          48        140   TRUE #> 2       0      0            100      138           0          0  FALSE # Cumulated useful nutrients plan$totals #>    N_useful P2O5_useful  K2O_useful  #>         188          48         140 # Target vs delivered alerts plan$alerts #> $N #> [1] "Eccesso" #>  #> $P2O5 #> [1] "Deficit" #>  #> $K2O #> [1] "OK" # ZVN 170 kg N/ha check plan$zvn_alert #> [1] "OK ZVN"

what: Precision agriculture: variable-rate from balance + NDVI
title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: NFert bridges agronomic balance (constant-rate dose, kg/ha) NDVI-based variable-rate application. integration logic : Compute field-average dose N_balance() + calculate_N_fertilization() scheda_N(). Use NDVI proxy crop vigour: low NDVI → N (rescue), high NDVI → less N (already vigorous). Apply one two NFert variable-rate algorithms: Calibration curve (estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve()): two-point three-point linear interpolation observed NDVI extremes user-defined N rates. Holland & Schepers (estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers()): sufficiency-index method using q95 NDVI reference base N rate. wrapper variable_rate_N() glues balance NDVI one call, preserves field-average dose rescaling (optionally) caps pixel MAS limit.

title: N, P and K fertilization with NFert — DPI Emilia-Romagna 2026
text: package implements Italian DPI Emilia-Romagna specification. Coefficients thresholds differ Italian regions national standards. plan_distribution() function checks totals vs balance targets ZVN 170 kg N/ha limit, yet check MAS per single application specific modality-cycle DPI constraints (cycle_modality.table). future release add checks. s2.rast dataset minimal Sentinel-2 NDVI raster used vignettes examples; production use, replace NDVI raster (Sentinel-2, drone, source) desired resolution. variable_rate_N() re-scales raster field-average matches agronomic dose; instead want allow mean drift (e.g. upper/lower envelopes explicitly chosen), use underlying estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve() / estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers() directly. crops multiple phases (e.g. grano duro fase nd), MAS lookup uses first matching row; pass phase = ... explicitly ambiguity matters.

title: Nitrogen Fertilization Management with NFert: A Comprehensive Guide
text: NFert R package provides comprehensive toolkit calculating nitrogen fertilization requirements field crops following Emilia-Romagna Regional Recommendations integrated production. vignette demonstrates use NFert nitrogen balance calculations, soil fertility assessment, precision agriculture applications.

title: Nitrogen Fertilization Management with NFert: A Comprehensive Guide
text: Nitrogen balance quantitative method accounts nitrogen inputs outputs agricultural system. general equation used NFert : Nitrogen Fertilization (N)=−B+C1+C2+D−E−Forg−G\text{Nitrogen Fertilization (N)} = - B + C1 + C2 + D - E - F_{org} - G : : Crop nitrogen requirements (kg/ha) B: Soil nitrogen supply (sum b1 b2) (kg/ha) C1: Nitrogen leaching loss winter (kg/ha) C2: Nitrogen leaching loss spring (kg/ha) D: Immobilization dispersion losses (kg/ha) E: Nitrogen previous crop residues (kg/ha) F_{org}: Nitrogen organic fertilizers (kg/ha) G: Natural nitrogen contributions (kg/ha)

title: Nitrogen Fertilization Management with NFert: A Comprehensive Guide
text: NFert implements method balance (Allegato 2) Disciplinari di Produzione Integrata (DPI) Emilia-Romagna, line Guida alla Fertilizzazione Minerale e Organica (DPI 2025) regional tool FertDPI / Fert_Office. References: DPI Norme Generali (Allegato 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 9), Reg. reg. 2/2024 3/2017, Direttiva Nitrati (91/676/CEE). Zone Vulnerabili ai Nitrati (ZVN) MAS (limiti di massima applicazione standard) binding 170 kg N/ha/year ceiling livestock effluents applies farm level. Formula N (Guida DPI 2025): N da apportare = − B + C + D − E − F − G = Fabbisogni colturali (assorbimenti unitari × produzione attesa). B = Fertilità del suolo: B1 (mineralizzazione S.O.: coefficiente × S.O. % × coefficiente tempo) + B2 (azoto pronto: coefficiente × N totale ‰; solo colture con ciclo < 1 anno). Per pluriennali B = B1 soltanto. C = Perdite per lisciviazione: Ca (autunno-inverno: <150 mm nessuna; 150–250 mm perdita progressiva; >250 mm tutto l’N pronto) + Cb (febbraio: 1 kg N/ha ogni 10 mm oltre 250 mm cumulati). D = Immobilizzazione e dispersione = B × fattore di correzione fc (funzione di ossigeno e tessitura: es. buona 0,15–0,25, moderata 0,20–0,30, impedita 0,30–0,40). E = N da residui della precessione (tabella: cereali paglia asportata −10, interrata −30; medica +80; soia 0; leguminose da granella +40; ecc.). F = N da fertilizzazioni organiche anni precedenti (ammendanti 50/30/20 %; Cattle slurry 30/15/10; suino/pollina 15/10/5). G = Apporti naturali (precipitazioni; fissazione per leguminose). NFert computes balance subtracts current-year organic N (Forg) obtain mineral N apply. Fractioning rules (max 100 kg/ha N per intervention herbaceous crops, 60 kg/ha tree crops; min 7 days interventions) presemina/autumn caps (e.g. max 30 kg/ha presemina, max 40 kg/ha arboree 15 Oct) set DPI must respected field plan. Soil texture (DPI groupings): 12 USDA texture classes grouped three macro-categories used efficiency coefficients: (1) Tendenzialmente sabbioso (S, SF, FS), (2) Franco / Medio impasto (L, F, FL, FSA, FA), (3) Tendenzialmente argilloso (FLA, , AL, ). NFert’s tri3() calc_soil_group_and_id_rag() provide texture soil group. Maximum allowed doses (MAS): crop ceiling N P₂O₅ must exceeded. Use get_MAS(crop) check_MAS(crop, N_planned) retrieve check MAS (see section ). Organic N efficiency (DPI): share organic N available crop year application depends fertilizer type, distribution technique (efficient / medium / low), soil texture. Efficient = injection, fertigation, incorporation within 4 h; medium = band spreading quick incorporation; low = surface spreading without incorporation. Ammendanti compostati use fixed 40% efficiency. NFert’s organic_fertilization() uses factors source frequency; DPI-specific efficiency soil distribution, refer FertDPI ‘Efficienza’ sheet regional guide.

title: Nitrogen Fertilization Management with NFert: A Comprehensive Guide
text: NFert provides following categories functions: Field-scale nitrogen balance - N_balance() plus ten component helpers (crop demand, soil supply, leaching, immobilisation, previous-crop residue, organic contributions, natural deposition). Phosphorus potassium balance - P_balance(), K_balance(). Distribution plan - plan_distribution() MAS 170 kg N ZVN checks. Precision agriculture - variable_rate_N(), estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve() estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers() VI-based VRT; build_strip_prescription() machine-width strips along -B line; export_prescription() / export_prescription_all() Shapefile, GeoJSON, KML, GeoPackage, John Deere, Trimble ISOXML TASKDATA formats. Remote-sensing diagnosis - compute_vi() (six VIs), compute_NNI() / compute_NNI_from_S2() / crop_params_NNI() GPR-based pipeline, nni_from_vi_empirical() single-VI regression path, estimate_biophysical() canopy trait retrieval. Farm-level workflow - farm_balance() iterates N_balance() every feature GeoJSON / Shapefile returns enriched sf layer ready export. Shiny application - run_app() opens six-tab web interface interactive forms, leaflet maps one-click export.

title: Nitrogen Fertilization Management with NFert: A Comprehensive Guide
code: # Create example NDVI raster set.seed(42) ndvi_raster <- raster(nrows=20, ncols=20, xmn=0, xmx=100, ymn=0, ymx=100) values(ndvi_raster) <- runif(ncell(ndvi_raster), 0.35, 0.85)  # Two-point calibration n_rate_2pt <- estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve(   raster = ndvi_raster,   minN = 40,  # N rate for max NDVI (healthy areas)   maxN = 80,  # N rate for min NDVI (areas needing more N)   calibration_type = "two-point",   plot = FALSE )  # Three-point calibration (more accurate) n_rate_3pt <- estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve(   raster = ndvi_raster,   minN = 40,   meanN = 60,  # N rate for mean NDVI   maxN = 80,   calibration_type = "three-point",   plot = FALSE )  # Compare results cat("Two-point calibration - Mean N rate:",      round(cellStats(n_rate_2pt, mean), 2), "kg/ha\n") #> Two-point calibration - Mean N rate: 60.05 kg/ha cat("Three-point calibration - Mean N rate:",      round(cellStats(n_rate_3pt, mean), 2), "kg/ha\n") #> Three-point calibration - Mean N rate: 60.03 kg/ha  # Spatial maps: NDVI and recommended N rate (variable-rate prescription) op <- par(no.readonly = TRUE) par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(2, 2, 2.5, 1)) raster::plot(ndvi_raster, main = "NDVI (example)", axes = FALSE,              col = grDevices::hcl.colors(100, "Greens", rev = TRUE)) raster::plot(n_rate_2pt, main = "N rate — 2-point (kg/ha)", axes = FALSE,              col = grDevices::hcl.colors(100, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE)) raster::plot(n_rate_3pt, main = "N rate — 3-point (kg/ha)", axes = FALSE,              col = grDevices::hcl.colors(100, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE)) par(op)

strong (grain)"),   ccp = c("Autumn-winter crop <150 days", "Spring-summer crop 100–130 days",           "Autumn-winter crop <150 days"),   clay = c(20, 30, 18),   sand = c(32, 28, 35),   Ntot = c(1.1, 1.6, 1.0),   SOM = c(1.9, 3.0, 1.7),   CN = c(8, 11, 9),   winter_rain = c(150, 180, 140),   prev_crop = c("Winter cereals straw removal", "Winter cereals straw removal",                 "Legumes"),   organic_quantity = c(0, 100, 80) )  # Function to process one field process_field <- function(row) {   N_balance(     expected_yield_tons_ha = row$expected_yield,     crop = as.character(row$crop),     ccp = as.character(row$ccp),     clay = row$clay,     sand = row$sand,     Ntot = row$Ntot,     SOM = row$SOM,     CN = row$CN,     winter_rain = row$winter_rain,     start_spring_rain = 40,     prev_crop = as.character(row$prev_crop),     source = "Cattle slurry",     fertorg_frequency = ifelse(row$organic_quantity > 0, "every year", "never"),     forg_quantity = row$organic_quantity,     oxygen_availability = "Normal",     location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas"   ) }  # Process all fields field_results <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(fields_data)), function(i) {   process_field(fields_data[i, ]) })  # Calculate N requirements fields_data$N_requirement <- sapply(field_results, function(x) {   calculate_N_fertilization(x) })  # Add field areas and calculate totals fields_data$area_ha <- c(15, 20, 12) fields_data$total_N_kg <- fields_data$N_requirement * fields_data$area_ha  # Summary table summary_table <- fields_data[, c("field_id", "crop", "area_ha",                                    "N_requirement", "total_N_kg")] names(summary_table)[4] <- "N_rate_kg_ha" print(summary_table) #>   field_id                           crop area_ha N_rate_kg_ha total_N_kg #> 1  Field_A Soft wheat FF - what: 3. Strip prescription along an A-B line
title: Farm-level balance and strip-prescription map
text: build_strip_prescription() tiles one plot parallel strips user-supplied working width. -B line given, longest side field used driving direction. Dose variability can driven uniform target, VI calibration curve, NNI zones, quantile classes.  Swapping VI calibration curve makes dose decrease vigour; mean across field preserved (mass-balance constraint) default. 2-D grid (variable dose cell--cell) produced passing cell_length > 0; explicit -B direction set angle_deg (degrees, 0 = east, 90 = north) supplying sf LINESTRING ab_line:
code: field <- farm[1, ]          # Campo Grande, 5.2 ha silage maize  # Option A: uniform 226 kg N/ha (from the balance), 24 m spreader rx_uni <- build_strip_prescription(   field         = field,   machine_width = 24,   variability   = "uniform",   n_target      = 226)  plot(sf::st_geometry(rx_uni), col = "#D5E4F0",      border = "#1F4E79", main = "Uniform strips, 24 m") # Option B: VI calibration (requires an NDVI raster) rx_cal <- build_strip_prescription(   field         = field,   machine_width = 24,   variability   = "calibration",   vi_raster     = my_ndvi_raster,   n_target      = 226,   min_dose      = 80,  max_dose = 260,   vi_low        = 0.35, vi_high = 0.80) # Option C: 24 m working width x 50 m along-strip cells, AB = 30 deg rx_grid <- build_strip_prescription(   field         = field,   machine_width = 24,   cell_length   = 50,   angle_deg     = 30,   variability   = "classes",   vi_raster     = my_ndvi_raster,   n_target      = 226,   min_dose      = 80, max_dose = 260,   n_classes     = 5)  # Option D: NNI-driven zones rx_nni <- build_strip_prescription(   field         = field,   machine_width = 24,   cell_length   = 0,           # continuous strips   variability   = "nni",   nni_raster    = my_nni_map,   n_target      = 226,   min_dose      = 80, max_dose = 260,   thr_lo        = 0.90, thr_hi = 1.10)

title: Prescription map worked example: NDVI, calibration curve, grid
text: real workflow read GeoTIFF UAV Sentinel-2. build plausible NDVI one line using internal demo helper (synthesiser used Shiny “Load demo NDVI” button):
code: library(raster)  # Two-patch NDVI with smooth base + noise set.seed(7) fp <- sf::st_transform(field, 3857) bb <- sf::st_bbox(fp)  nx <- 150 ny <- 110 r  <- raster(xmn = bb$xmin, xmx = bb$xmax,              ymn = bb$ymin, ymx = bb$ymax,              ncols = nx, nrows = ny, crs = 3857)  x  <- seq(0, 1, length.out = nx); y <- seq(0, 1, length.out = ny) gx <- matrix(x, nrow = ny, ncol = nx, byrow = TRUE) gy <- matrix(y, nrow = ny, ncol = nx) base   <- 0.72 + 0.05 * sin(4 * gx) + 0.04 * cos(5 * gy) patch1 <- 0.22 * exp(-(((gx - 0.30)^2 + (gy - 0.35)^2) / 0.010)) patch2 <- 0.26 * exp(-(((gx - 0.70)^2 + (gy - 0.55)^2) / 0.008)) ndvi_vals <- base - patch1 - patch2 + 0.03 * rnorm(nx * ny) ndvi_vals <- pmin(pmax(ndvi_vals, 0.30), 0.92) raster::values(r) <- ndvi_vals r <- raster::mask(r, as(fp, "Spatial"))  names(r) <- "NDVI" r #> class      : RasterLayer  #> dimensions : 110, 150, 16500  (nrow, ncol, ncell) #> resolution : 3.116946, 4.010757  (x, y) #> extent     : 1082192, 1082660, 5629081, 5629522  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> crs        : +proj=merc +a=6378137 +b=6378137 +lat_ts=0 +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +k=1 +units=m +nadgrids=@null +wktext +no_defs  #> source     : memory #> names      : NDVI  #> values     : 0.3834043, 0.901509  (min, max)  plot(r, main = "Input NDVI (synthetic)",      col = hcl.colors(30, "YlGn"))

title: Prescription map worked example: NDVI, calibration curve, grid
text: two-point linear calibration maps NDVI reference (lowest vigour) max_dose (push canopy development) high-vigour NDVI min_dose (canopy already lush, less N needed):
code: # Agronomic N target from the field-scale balance (example: 220 kg N/ha # for an 11 t/ha grain maize in Pianura Padana) N_target <- 220 min_dose <- 60 max_dose <- 260  vi_low  <- 0.35    # anchor for max_dose vi_high <- 0.80    # anchor for min_dose  # Plot the calibration vi  <- seq(0.20, 0.95, length.out = 200) slope <- (min_dose - max_dose) / (vi_high - vi_low) intercept <- max_dose - slope * vi_low dose_curve <- pmin(pmax(slope * vi + intercept, min_dose), max_dose)  plot(vi, dose_curve, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#1F4E79",      xlab = "NDVI", ylab = "N rate (kg N/ha)",      main = "Two-point calibration curve") abline(v = c(vi_low, vi_high), lty = 3, col = "#888") abline(h = c(min_dose, max_dose), lty = 3, col = "#888") points(c(vi_low, vi_high), c(max_dose, min_dose),        pch = 19, col = "#C0392B") text(vi_low,  max_dose, sprintf("(NDVI=%.2f, N=%d)", vi_low, max_dose),      pos = 4, cex = 0.9, col = "#C0392B") text(vi_high, min_dose, sprintf("(NDVI=%.2f, N=%d)", vi_high, min_dose),      pos = 2, cex = 0.9, col = "#C0392B")

title: Prescription map worked example: NDVI, calibration curve, grid
text: variable_rate_N() applies calibration every pixel rescales output area-weighted mean equals balance target (mass-balance constraint). method = "calibration" two anchor points taken automatically empirical min / max NDVI raster mapped (maxN, minN) — .e. lowest-vigour pixels get highest dose vice-versa. need fix anchors (e.g. force vi_low = 0.35 vi_high = 0.80 local calibration), use Option B build_strip_prescription() , exposes arguments.  %||% just defensive code (versions variable_rate_N() return list, others raster directly); defined top vignette hidden setup chunk.
code: vr <- variable_rate_N(   ndvi_raster = r,   n_dose      = N_target,   method      = "calibration",      # or "holland" for Holland & Schepers   minN        = min_dose,   maxN        = max_dose )  rx_raster <- vr$rate_raster %||% vr    # robust to return shape plot(rx_raster,      main = "VRT raster (kg N/ha)",      col = rev(hcl.colors(30, "RdYlGn"))) cat("Mean applied N:  ", round(cellStats(rx_raster, "mean", na.rm = TRUE), 1),     "kg N/ha\n") #> Mean applied N:   220 kg N/ha cat("Range:           ", round(cellStats(rx_raster, "min", na.rm = TRUE), 1),     "-", round(cellStats(rx_raster, "max", na.rm = TRUE), 1), "kg N/ha\n") #> Range:            101.5 - 439.8 kg N/ha

what: 5. Option B - machine-width grid along an A-B line
title: Prescription map worked example: NDVI, calibration curve, grid
text: pixel--pixel raster Option fine self-driving tractor continuous-rate spreader, many farm monitors prefer block prescription: rectangular cells machine_width x cell_length single dose per cell. NFert’s build_strip_prescription() produces exactly , applying calibration internally:  -B direction inferred longest edge field polygon. Pass angle_deg = 45 (number) force specific driving direction, ab_line = <sf LINESTRING> use existing tramline.
code: rx_grid <- build_strip_prescription(   field         = field,   machine_width = 24,     # metres, working width of the spreader   cell_length   = 50,     # metres, along the A-B line (0 = continuous strips)   angle_deg     = NULL,   # NULL = auto-detect long side; set 30 / 90 / ... to force   variability   = "calibration",   vi_raster     = r,   n_target      = N_target,   min_dose      = min_dose,   max_dose      = max_dose,   vi_low        = vi_low,   vi_high       = vi_high,   preserve_mean = TRUE)  # Plot with the dose-based palette used by the Shiny app pal <- colorRampPalette(c("#2F7A44", "#F6E7C7", "#C0392B"))(100) col_idx <- as.integer(round(   (rx_grid$dose - min(rx_grid$dose, na.rm = TRUE)) /     diff(range(rx_grid$dose, na.rm = TRUE)) * 99)) + 1 plot(sf::st_geometry(sf::st_transform(rx_grid, 3857)),      col = pal[col_idx], border = "#333", lwd = 0.6,      main = sprintf("Grid prescription - %d cells, 24 m x 50 m",                     nrow(rx_grid)))

text: Mass-balance constraint - preserve_mean = TRUE (default) makes area-weighted mean dose match n_target. balance provides legally defensible field ceiling (MAS / ZVN 170 kg N/ha), VRT / grid layer redistributes space. Calibration sources - default anchors (vi_low = 0.35, vi_high = 0.80) work mid-season Sentinel-2 NDVI Northern Italy. Recalibrate locally set plot-level yield / N-status samples operational use. Cell granularity - finer cell_length gives denser grid risks delivery oscillations spreader; 50-100 m along -B line reasonable default 24-36 m machine. pipeline NNI - replace variability = "calibration" variability = "nni" pass NNI raster (compute_NNI_from_S2() nni_from_vi_empirical()) instead VI drive strips nitrogen status zones directly.

title: Remote Sensing Diagnosis: multi-index VIs and Nitrogen Nutrition Index
text: vignette demonstrates remote-sensing diagnosis pipeline added NFert 0.7.0: Compute multiple vegetation indices (VIs) multi-band raster compute_vi(). Diagnose crop nitrogen status pixel--pixel compute_NNI() / diagnose_N_status(), using species-specific critical nitrogen dilution curve Lemaire & Gastal (1997). Feed diagnosis variable-rate prescription pipeline (variable_rate_N()) applied dose responds NNI diagnosis rather raw vegetation index alone. matters: NDVI saturates LAI > 3–4. mid- late-vegetative stages (GS30+ winter wheat; V8+ maize) red-edge indices (NDRE, CIred-edge) far sensitive canopy N status. indices still just proxies: scientifically correct diagnostic variable NNI, uses dilution curve convert biomass + N% dimensionless deficiency / sufficiency index.

title: Remote Sensing Diagnosis: multi-index VIs and Nitrogen Nutrition Index
text: compute_vi() computes NDVI, NDRE, GNDVI, CIred-edge, MCARI, MSAVI2 stack, configurable band mapping. Default mapping follows Sentinel-2 L2A naming.  NDVI GNDVI typically cluster narrow range closed canopies (saturation); NDRE CIred-edge preserve variability high biomass. red-edge indices reference N diagnosis GS30+ / V8+.
code: ndvi  <- compute_vi(s2, "NDVI") ndre  <- compute_vi(s2, "NDRE") gndvi <- compute_vi(s2, "GNDVI") cire  <- compute_vi(s2, "CIred")  summary(getValues(ndvi)) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.6329  0.7314  0.7662  0.7655  0.8055  0.8615 summary(getValues(ndre)) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.1751  0.3596  0.4406  0.4456  0.5384  0.6886 par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(2, 2, 2, 3)) plot(ndvi,  main = "NDVI",  col = rev(terrain.colors(30))) plot(ndre,  main = "NDRE",  col = rev(terrain.colors(30))) plot(gndvi, main = "GNDVI", col = rev(terrain.colors(30))) plot(cire,  main = "CIred", col = rev(terrain.colors(30)))

title: Remote Sensing Diagnosis: multi-index VIs and Nitrogen Nutrition Index
text: critical_N_curve() returns (, b, W_min) coefficients species-specific critical nitrogen dilution curve. Italian synonyms accepted. curve Nc = * W^(-b), Nc critical N concentration (% DM) W aboveground dry biomass (t DM / ha). Defaults come Justes 1994 (wheat), Plenet & Lemaire 2000 (maize), Sheehy 1998 (rice), Colnenne 1998 (rapeseed), Duru 1997 (grass), van Oosterom 2010 (sorghum), Debaeke 2012 (sunflower).
code: critical_N_curve("wheat") #> $a #> [1] 5.35 #>  #> $b #> [1] 0.44 #>  #> $W_min #> [1] 1.55 #>  #> $reference #> [1] "Justes et al. 1994" critical_N_curve("mais")   # italian #> $a #> [1] 3.4 #>  #> $b #> [1] 0.37 #>  #> $W_min #> [1] 1 #>  #> $reference #> [1] "Plenet & Lemaire 2000"

title: Remote Sensing Diagnosis: multi-index VIs and Nitrogen Nutrition Index
text: usual variable_rate_N() accepts normalised VI. diagnosis-guided upgrade : () use red-edge index VRT, (b) zero prescription NNI diagnosis shows excessive N status, fertiliser applied crop already -supplied.
code:
N_target <- 160   # e.g. from a preliminary N_balance()  vr <- variable_rate_N(ndre, n_dose = N_target,                       method = "holland",                       minN = 60, maxN = 200) #> Warning in estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers(ndvi_raster = ndvi_raster, : #> Raster projection information missing. Ensure NDVI values are in 0-1 range.  # Mask out pixels classified as "excessive" (class == 3) rate_guided <- vr$rate_raster rate_guided[d$class == 3] <- 0  par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 4)) plot(vr$rate_raster,      main = "VRT (NDRE + Holland)",      col  = rev(heat.colors(30))) plot(rate_guided,      main = "VRT masked by NNI (class 3 -> 0)",      col  = rev(heat.colors(30))) cat("Full prescription  mean :",     round(cellStats(vr$rate_raster, mean), 1), "kg/ha
") #> Full prescription  mean : 160 kg/ha cat("NNI-guided        mean  :",     round(cellStats(rate_guided, mean), 1), "kg/ha
") #> NNI-guided        mean  : 103.9 kg/ha cat("N saved by NNI mask     :",     round(cellStats(vr$rate_raster - rate_guided, mean), 1),     "kg/ha avg, or",     round(cellStats((vr$rate_raster - rate_guided), sum) *           prod(res(rate_guided)) / 10000, 1),     "kg total over the field
") #> N saved by NNI mask     : 56.1 kg/ha avg, or 56.1 kg total over the field

what: 7. Empirical NNI from a single VI raster (no GPR models)
title: Remote Sensing Diagnosis: multi-index VIs and Nitrogen Nutrition Index
text: full compute_NNI_from_S2() pipeline requires 8 pyeogpr .json model files plus 10-band Sentinel-2 L2A scene. single VI raster available (UAV NDRE, satellite NDVI, etc.), helper nni_from_vi_empirical() delivers NNI map directly published linear regression: Default slope intercept first-guess values Cao 2013 (rice), Fitzgerald 2010 Magney 2017 (wheat), Li 2014 (maize). Local recalibration 30-50 plot-level ground-truth samples strongly recommended.
code: ndre <- raster::raster("ndre_field.tif") out  <- nni_from_vi_empirical(   ndre,   index = "NDRE",       # or "NDVI", "CIred_edge"   crop  = "wheat",      # wheat / maize / rice / barley   # slope = 3.5, intercept = 0.30,  # override with local calibration   nni_thresholds = c(0.90, 1.10))  raster::plot(out$NNI,   main = "NNI (empirical)") raster::plot(out$zones, main = "Zones (1 deficient / 2 optimal / 3 excessive)")

title: Spatial Nitrogen Balance and Variable-Rate Prescription
text: vignette demonstrates full NFert spatial workflow: Load real soil-property rasters (bundled package) Compute spatially-explicit nitrogen balance spatial_N_balance() Generate variable-rate (VRT) prescription variable_rate_N() (Holland & Schepers method) workflow implements pipeline described Section 2.2 NFert SoftwareX article: field-scale balance provides regulatory-compliant N target, VRT functions redistribute space mass-balance constraint.

title: Spatial Nitrogen Balance and Variable-Rate Prescription
text: NFert ships six GeoTIFF rasters 12-ha arable field near Cremona (Northern Italy), ~3.5 m resolution: total nitrogen (TN, %), soil organic matter (SOM, %), clay, sand silt content (%), C/N ratio.
code: library(NFert) library(raster) #> Loading required package: sp  ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "NFert")  # Read individually: the bundled GeoTIFFs may have sub-pixel extent # mismatches from separate acquisitions, so we resample to a common # grid (the TN raster) before stacking. r_tn   <- raster::raster(file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_TN.tif")) r_som  <- raster::raster(file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_SOM.tif")) r_clay <- raster::raster(file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_Clay.tif")) r_sand <- raster::raster(file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_Sand.tif")) r_cn   <- raster::raster(file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_CNratio.tif"))  align <- function(r, ref) {   if (!raster::compareRaster(r, ref, stopiffalse = FALSE)) {     r <- raster::resample(r, ref, method = "bilinear")   }   r } soil <- raster::stack(r_tn,                       align(r_som,  r_tn),                       align(r_clay, r_tn),                       align(r_sand, r_tn),                       align(r_cn,   r_tn)) names(soil) <- c("TN", "SOM", "Clay", "Sand", "CNratio")  soil #> class      : RasterStack  #> dimensions : 96, 101, 9696, 5  (nrow, ncol, ncell, nlayers) #> resolution : 3.486166e-05, 3.499425e-05  (x, y) #> extent     : 9.995467, 9.998988, 45.04422, 45.04758  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> crs        : +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs  #> names      :      TN,     SOM,    Clay,     Sand, CNratio  #> min values :       ?,       ?,       ?, 7.151633,       ?  #> max values :       ?,       ?,       ?, 24.84696,       ? par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 4)) plot(soil[["SOM"]], main = "SOM (%)", col = terrain.colors(30)) plot(soil[["Clay"]], main = "Clay (%)", col = heat.colors(30))

title: Spatial Nitrogen Balance and Variable-Rate Prescription
text: spatial_N_balance() iterates N_balance() every non-NA pixel stack. Agronomic climatic parameters (crop, yield, rainfall, previous crop, organic history) uniform; spatial variability driven soil rasters alone.  field-average N apply :
code: n_map <- spatial_N_balance(   soil_stack            = soil,   expected_yield_tons_ha = 60,   crop                  = "Silage maize (class 700)",   ccp                   = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days",   oxygen_availability   = "Normal",   winter_rain           = 160,   start_spring_rain     = 40,   prev_crop             = "Winter cereals straw removal",   source                = "Cattle slurry",   fertorg_frequency     = "every year",   location              = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",   forg_quantity         = 100 )  n_map #> class      : RasterStack  #> dimensions : 96, 101, 9696, 10  (nrow, ncol, ncell, nlayers) #> resolution : 3.486166e-05, 3.499425e-05  (x, y) #> extent     : 9.995467, 9.998988, 45.04422, 45.04758  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> crs        : +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs  #> names      :           A,           B,          C1,          C2,           D,           E,           F,        Forg,           G,  N_to_apply  #> min values : 234.0000000,  31.9104424,  20.0000000,   0.4793168,  19.5731327,   0.0000000,   0.0000000,   0.3000000,  13.4000000, 225.8138077  #> max values : 234.0000000,  35.6650130,  20.0000000,   0.5108526,  20.6995039,   0.0000000,   0.0000000,   0.3000000,  13.4000000, 228.4735429 plot(n_map[["N_to_apply"]],      main = "Mineral N to apply (kg N/ha)",      col = rev(terrain.colors(30))) cellStats(n_map[["N_to_apply"]], stat = "mean") #> [1] 227.2872

title: Spatial Nitrogen Balance and Variable-Rate Prescription
text: real application NDVI raster comes UAV satellite imagery. simulate one correlated SOM (higher SOM → better canopy vigor):
code: set.seed(42) som_vals <- values(soil[["SOM"]]) som_01   <- (som_vals - min(som_vals, na.rm = TRUE)) /             diff(range(som_vals, na.rm = TRUE)) ndvi_vals <- 0.52 + 0.25 * som_01 + rnorm(length(som_01), 0, 0.025) ndvi_vals <- pmin(pmax(ndvi_vals, 0.35), 0.85) ndvi_vals[is.na(som_vals)] <- NA  ndvi <- raster(soil[["SOM"]]) values(ndvi) <- ndvi_vals names(ndvi) <- "NDVI"

title: Spatial Nitrogen Balance and Variable-Rate Prescription
text: VRT function redistributes field-mean N target across NDVI-based vigor gradient, mass-balance constraint described Section 2.2 article:
code: N_target <- cellStats(n_map[["N_to_apply"]], stat = "mean")  vr <- variable_rate_N(   ndvi_raster = ndvi,   n_dose      = N_target,   method      = "holland",   minN        = 40,   maxN        = 180 )  rx <- vr$rate_raster par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 4)) plot(ndvi, main = "Synthetic NDVI", col = rev(terrain.colors(30))) plot(rx,   main = "VRT N prescription (kg N/ha)",      col = rev(heat.colors(30)))

title: N, P and K fertilization following Emilia-Romagna DPI 2026
text: NFert R package calculating nitrogen, phosphorus potassium fertilization requirements field tree crops following Disciplinari di Produzione Integrata (DPI) Emilia-Romagna (ed. 2026). implements method balance (Allegato 2) Scheda dose Standard method, mirroring official Fert_Office tool (v1.26, Febbraio 2026). supports maximum allowed doses (MAS), soil texture grouping, Olsen P exchangeable K availability, fertilization distribution planning (organic + mineral), end--cycle soil P K estimation precision agriculture (NDVI-based variable rate).

title: N, P and K fertilization following Emilia-Romagna DPI 2026
text: DPI Emilia-Romagna (2025–2026) – Norme Generali; Allegato 2 (metodo del bilancio), Allegati 3, 4, 6, 7, 9; Guida alla Fertilizzazione Minerale e Organica (N, P, K) FertDPI / Fert_Office – strumento regionale; NFert riproduce la logica del bilancio azotato per uso R Reg. reg. 2/2024 – limiti MAS (Allegato 9); Reg. reg. 3/2017 – utilizzazione agronomica degli effluenti e del digestato Direttiva Nitrati (91/676/CEE) – ZVN limite 170 kg N/ha/anno da effluenti zootecnici; Reg. UE 2021/2115 (soglie minime efficienza)

title: N, P and K fertilization following Emilia-Romagna DPI 2026
text: Nitrogen balance (Allegato 2): N_balance() (crop demand), B1/B2 (soil fertility), C1/C2 (leaching), D (immobilization), E (previous crop residues), F (previous years’ organics), Forg (current year organics), G (natural deposition). Supports soil_seeding = "-till" (−3 kg/ha b1), greenhouse = TRUE (+2 D), automatic fold negative E D. Phosphorus balance: P_balance() Olsen P classification (classify_P_olsen()), Arricchimento / Mantenimento / Riduzione strategy, texture-dependent weight 30 cm P immobilisation factor 1.6. Potassium balance: K_balance() K availability class texture (classify_K()), clay-driven leaching (K_leaching_by_clay()). Scheda dose Standard: scheda_N() scheda_PK() DPI 2026 catalogue decrements increments MAS cap N. Fertilization distribution plan: plan_distribution() combines organic matrices (DPI 2026 N efficiency soil × dose × sector) mineral fertilisers (146-entry concimi.table), returns Eccesso/Deficit alerts ZVN 170 kg N/ha check. End--cycle soil dotation: estimate_soil_P_end_of_cycle() estimate_soil_K_end_of_cycle() next-year planning. Soil chemistry: classify_pH(), classify_carbonate_tot(), classify_carbonate_att(), classify_CEC(), ratio_Mg_K(), ratio_K_CEC(), classify_SOM(), max_SO_input(). MAS (Massimali): get_MAS() check_MAS() maximum allowed N per crop (DPI 2026, RR 2/2024). Precision agriculture: NDVI-based variable rate (calibration curve Holland & Schepers).

title: N, P and K fertilization following Emilia-Romagna DPI 2026
text: N da apportare = + C + D − B − E − F − G : Fabbisogno della coltura (resa × coefficiente asportazione) B: Fertilità del suolo (N mineralizzabile dalla S.O. + N pronto) C: Perdite per lisciviazione (C1 autunno-inverno, C2 fine inverno) D: Immobilizzazione e dispersione E: N da precessione colturale (residui, leguminose, paglie) F: N da fertilizzazione organiche anni precedenti G: Apporti naturali (precipitazioni) NFert computes balance subtracts current-year organic N (Forg) give mineral N apply. Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (ZVN), 170 kg N/ha/year limit livestock effluents applies farm level.

text: Calculates nitrogen balance crop considering various soil environmental factors.

title: Nitrogen Balance Calculation — N_balance
code: N_balance(   expected_yield_tons_ha = 15,   crop = "Grano tenero FF (granella)",   ccp = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days",   sand = 50,   clay = 35,   Ntot = 1.2,   SOM = 1.2,   CN = 9.5,   oxygen_availability = "Normal",   winter_rain = 160,   start_spring_rain = 40,   prev_crop = "Winter cereals straw removal",   source = "Cattle slurry",   fertorg_frequency = "every year",   location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",   forg_quantity = 100,   organic_previous_year_N = 0,   soil_group = NULL,   distribution_efficiency = NULL,   soil_seeding = c("traditional", "no-till"),   greenhouse = FALSE,   E_to_D = TRUE )

title: Nitrogen Balance Calculation — N_balance
text: expected_yield_tons_ha Expected crop yield tons per hectare. crop Name crop. ccp Crop calendar period (e.g., "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days"). sand Percentage sand soil. clay Percentage clay soil. Ntot Total nitrogen content soil (%). SOM Soil organic matter content (%). CN Carbon nitrogen ratio soil. oxygen_availability Oxygen availability level soil (e.g., "Normal"). winter_rain Total winter rainfall (mm). start_spring_rain Rainfall start spring (mm). prev_crop Previous crop grown field. source Source organic fertilizer (e.g., "Cattle slurry"). fertorg_frequency Frequency organic fertilizer application (e.g., "every year"). location Location field relative urban areas. forg_quantity Quantity organic fertilizer applied (m3/ha t/ha, depending source). Use 0 organic application: Forg set 0 without calling organic_fertilization(). organic_previous_year_N Optional. Total N (kg/ha) organic fertilization applied previous year (source/frequency). provided, term F (residual N previous years' organic, DPI section 3.1.6) included. Default 0. soil_group Optional. Soil group (e.g. calc_soil_group_and_id_rag()) use DPI efficiency current-year organic. set, distribution_efficiency also set. distribution_efficiency Optional. "efficient", "medium", "low" (DPI distribution). Used soil_group DPI organic N efficiency. soil_seeding One "traditional" (default) "-till". "-till", DPI 2026 applies 3 kg/ha reduction b1 (readily available N). greenhouse Logical. TRUE, 2 kg/ha added D (DPI 2026 greenhouse factor). Default FALSE. E_to_D Logical. TRUE (default), negative E (e.g. buried stalks = -40) added D Fert_Office v1.26 sheet C&D, E output set 0. FALSE, E stays negative balance uses calculate_N_fertilization().

title: Nitrogen Balance Calculation — N_balance
text: data frame containing calculated nitrogen balance components (kg/ha): : Total nitrogen demand crop (kg/ha). B: Total soil nitrogen supply (sum b1 b2) (kg/ha). b1: Readily available mineral nitrogen (DPI B2) (kg/ha). b2: Mineralizable N soil organic matter (DPI B1) (kg/ha). C1: Nitrogen leaching loss winter (kg/ha). C2: Nitrogen leaching loss spring (kg/ha). D: Nitrogen immobilization loss (kg/ha). Includes abs(E) contribution E_to_D = TRUE E < 0, +2 greenhouse = TRUE. E: Nitrogen previous crop residues (kg/ha). E_to_D = TRUE raw value negative, column reports 0 (moved D). F: Nitrogen previous years' organic fertilization (kg/ha). Forg: Nitrogen organic fertilizer (current year, efficient N) (kg/ha). G: Natural nitrogen contribution (e.g., rainfall) (kg/ha). surplus_pluviometrico: Logical flag (DPI 2026 standard sheet, surplus rain).

title: Nitrogen Balance Calculation — N_balance
text: nitrogen fertilization requirement (DPI formula) : N_fert = - B + C1 + C2 + D - E - F - Forg - G

title: Nitrogen Balance Calculation — N_balance
code: N_balance(expected_yield_tons_ha = 15, crop = "Mais trinciato (classe 700)",           ccp = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days", sand = 50, clay = 35,           Ntot = 1.2, SOM = 1.2, CN = 9.5, oxygen_availability = "Normal",           winter_rain = 160, start_spring_rain = 40,           prev_crop = "Winter cereals straw removal", source = "Cattle slurry",           fertorg_frequency = "every year", location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",           forg_quantity = 100) #>      A      B    b1    b2 C1    C2       D E F Forg    G surplus_pluviometrico #> 1 58.5 38.808 29.16 9.648 20 2.916 21.6424 0 0  0.3 13.4                 FALSE

text: users rather call end--end compute_NNI_from_S2 wrapper, handles crop-specific parameters, FVC / SCL masking zone classification single call. function exposed advanced workflows need direct control conversion. conversions follow $$W_{leaf}\ (\mathrm{g\ m^{-2}}) = LAI \cdot C_m \cdot 10^4$$ $$W\ (\mathrm{t\ DM\ ha^{-1}}) = W_{leaf} / (100 \cdot \alpha_{leaf})$$ (leaf--total aboveground allocation coefficient \(\alpha_{leaf}\), ~0.30-0.45 depending crop phenology), $$N_{actual}(\% DM) = 100 \cdot N_{total} / W_{leaf} \cdot \alpha_{leaf}^{-1}$$ \(N_{total}\) either equal CNC_Cprot (g N m\(^{-2}\)) k * CNC_Cab chlorophyll path used. Pixels W < w_min set NA critical-N dilution curve defined biomass.

title: Build a strip-by-strip prescription map from a field polygon — build_strip_prescription
text: field sf POLYGON (single feature) field boundary. machine_width Numeric, working width spreader / spray boom metres (default 24). cell_length Optional numeric. supplied >0, every strip subdivided rectangular cells length (metres) along -B direction, producing true 2-D prescription grid. NULL 0, strip left single polygon (classic "strip map"). angle_deg Optional numeric. Azimuth -B line degrees (0 = east, 90 = north). Overrides ab_line supplied. ab_line Optional sf LINESTRING giving driving direction. ab_line angle_deg NULL, long side field used. variability One "uniform", "calibration", "nni", "classes". vi_raster terra::SpatRaster raster::RasterLayer vegetation index (0-1). Required "calibration" "classes". nni_raster , NNI values. Required "nni". n_target Target field-level mean dose (kg N ha\(^{-1}\)). min_dose, max_dose Numeric caps (kg N ha\(^{-1}\)). vi_low, vi_high Two-point calibration anchors (variability = "calibration"). vi_low dose capped max_dose; vi_high min_dose. thr_lo, thr_hi NNI thresholds (variability = "nni"). n_classes Integer number equal-spaced dose classes variability = "classes" (default 5). preserve_mean Logical. TRUE (default) strip- level dose rescaled area-weighted mean matches n_target. Guarantees mass-balance compliance balance-based field ceiling. crs_metric EPSG code metric CRS used internally (default 3857, Web Mercator). Use local UTM higher accuracy larger fields.

title: Build a strip-by-strip prescription map from a field polygon — build_strip_prescription
text: sf object strip polygons columns strip_id, dose (kg N ha\(^{-1}\)), area_ha, mean_vi mean_nni available. CRS matches input.

title: Build a strip-by-strip prescription map from a field polygon — build_strip_prescription
text: builder works entirely metric CRS (Web Mercator default); final sf object returned original CRS input field downstream tools see consistent coordinates.

title: Build a strip-by-strip prescription map from a field polygon — build_strip_prescription
text: "uniform" Every strip receives n_target dose. Useful baseline. "calibration" Samples mean vi_raster value per strip maps dose via two- three-point calibration curve. dose decreases vigour (higher VI = less N). "nni" Samples mean nni_raster value per strip translates three agronomic zones Lemaire & Gastal: deficient (NNI < thr_lo) gets max_dose, optimal (thr_lo <= NNI <= thr_hi) gets n_target, excessive (NNI > thr_hi) gets min_dose. "classes" Assigns doses equal seq(min_dose, max_dose, length.= n_classes) based k-quantile class mean vi_raster value per strip. Useful categorical map preferred. Regardless method, mean dose across field rescaled match n_target (mass-balance constraint) preserve_mean = TRUE (default).

title: Build a strip-by-strip prescription map from a field polygon — build_strip_prescription
text: ab_line NULL, -B direction taken longest edge polygon's minimum-area bounding rectangle (via sf::st_minimum_rotated_rectangle()). typically recovers "long side" convention farmers use field. ab_line sf LINESTRING, first segment defines driving direction.

title: Calculate Nitrogen Fertilization Requirement — calculate_N_fertilization
text: function calculates final nitrogen fertilization requirement based nitrogen balance components. result negative, returns 0 (fertilization needed). standard (DPI) formula :   : : Crop nitrogen demand B: Soil nitrogen supply C1, C2: Leaching losses D: Immobilization losses E: Nitrogen previous crop residues F: Nitrogen previous years' organic fertilization (0 balance) Forg: Nitrogen organic fertilizer (current year) G: Natural nitrogen contribution

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
text: Computes Nitrogen Nutrition Index, standard scalar indicator crop N status (Lemaire & Gastal 1997). NNI = N_actual / N_c, N_actual measured aboveground N concentration (% DM) N_c critical N concentration derived species-specific dilution curve \(N_c = \cdot W^{-b}\).

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
code: compute_NNI(N_content, biomass, crop, curve = NULL, is_percent = FALSE)

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
text: N_content Aboveground N concentration fraction DM (e.g. 0.025 = 2.5pct) percentage DM (pass is_percent = TRUE). biomass Aboveground dry biomass t DM / ha (tonnes). crop Crop identifier, passed critical_N_curve. curve Optional list b (optionally W_min) override default curve. Useful custom local calibration. is_percent Logical. N_content supplied percentage (e.g. 2.5 2.5pct) rather fraction? Default FALSE (fraction). Auto-detected warned values > 1.

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
text: numeric scalar / vector RasterLayer NNI values.

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
text: Interpretation: NNI < deficient_threshold (default 0.90): N-deficient deficient_threshold <= NNI <= excessive_threshold (0.90 1.10 default): optimal / balanced NNI > excessive_threshold (default 1.10): luxury consumption / N-excess three arguments can scalar, numeric vectors length (pixel-wise / plot-wise), raster::RasterLayers aligned grid. RasterLayer, output RasterLayer; otherwise numeric vector returned.

title: Nitrogen Nutrition Index (NNI) — compute_NNI
text: Lemaire, G. & Gastal, F. (1997). N uptake distribution plant canopies. : Lemaire G. (ed.) Diagnosis Nitrogen Status Crops. Springer, Berlin. Lemaire, G., Jeuffroy, M.-H., Gastal, F. (2008). text: named list SpatRaster layers: W (t DM ha\(^{-1}\)) – aboveground biomass N_actual (pct DM) – plant N concentration N_crit (pct DM) – critical N dilution curve NNI – Nitrogen Nutrition Index (dimensionless) zones – integer raster (1 deficient / 2 optimal / 3 excessive) mask – logical raster retained pixels Returned items NULL return_intermediates = FALSE intermediate variable requested.

title: End-to-end Nitrogen Nutrition Index pipeline from Sentinel-2 L2A — compute_NNI_from_S2
text: pipeline implements classical Lemaire & Gastal (1997) formulation: $$W_{leaf} = LAI \cdot C_m \cdot 10^4  \quad (\mathrm{g\ m^{-2}})$$ $$W = W_{leaf} / \alpha_{leaf}  \quad (\mathrm{g\ m^{-2}})$$ $$N_{total} = k \cdot CNC\_Cab  \quad \mathrm{}  \quad N_{total} = CNC\_Cprot$$ $$N_{actual}(\%) = 100 \cdot N_{total} / W$$ $$N_c(\%) = \cdot (W_{tha})^{-b}$$ $$NNI = N_{actual} / N_c$$ \(\alpha_{leaf}\) leaf--total aboveground allocation coefficient \(k\) N--chlorophyll ratio (crop-specific, see crop_params_NNI). \(W_{tha}\) W rescaled t DM ha\(^{-1}\). Two N-content pathways supported: Protein path (cnc_layer = "CNC_Cprot", default). Uses GPR retrieval trained canopy N protein content directly — internally consistent path, extra coefficient needed. Chlorophyll path (cnc_layer = "CNC_Cab"). Uses canopy chlorophyll retrieval multiplies canopy-level N:Chl ratio \(k\) (default ~55-60, requires local calibration — see crop_params_NNI). Preferred CNC_Cab best-trained model target crop calibration work already done.

title: End-to-end Nitrogen Nutrition Index pipeline from Sentinel-2 L2A — compute_NNI_from_S2
text: Two masks applied NNI computation: fvc raster supplied, pixels FVC < crop_params$fvc_min removed (default threshold 0.50). level BRDF contaminated soil background GPR retrievals degrade. Pixels W crop_params$w_min masked critical-N dilution curve defined seedling stage (default 1 t DM ha\(^{-1}\)). Optional Sentinel-2 SCL (Scene Classification Layer) classes can dropped via scl_keep (vector valid classes; defaults standard vegetation-safe classes 4, 5, 6, 7 = VEGETATION, NOT_VEGETATED, WATER, UNCLASSIFIED).

title: End-to-end Nitrogen Nutrition Index pipeline from Sentinel-2 L2A — compute_NNI_from_S2
text: NNI discretised three classes following Lemaire & Gastal (1997): Thresholds user-configurable via nni_thresholds argument.

title: Compute a vegetation index from a multi-band raster — compute_vi
text: stack raster::RasterStack RasterBrick required spectral bands. Band names (layer indices) mapped bands. index One "NDVI", "NDRE", "GNDVI", "CIred", "MCARI", "MSAVI2" (case-insensitive). bands Named list character vector mapping index inputs (red, red_edge, nir, green) layer names stack. Defaults Sentinel-2 L2A naming (red="B04", red_edge="B05", nir="B08", green="B03"). bands required chosen index looked . scale_factor Numeric. input reflectance stored integer DN (e.g. Sentinel-2 L2A 0–10000), divide factor computing index. Default 1 (reflectance already 0–1). clamp Logical. TRUE (default bounded indices NDVI, NDRE, GNDVI, MSAVI2), clamp output range -1 1. Ignored CIred MCARI (unbounded).

title: Compute a vegetation index from a multi-band raster — compute_vi
text: raster::RasterLayer computed index. Layer name set index name.

title: Compute a vegetation index from a multi-band raster — compute_vi
text: Index formulas (references References section): NDVI (NIR minus Red) divided (NIR plus Red). Rouse 1974. NDRE (NIR minus RedEdge) divided (NIR plus RedEdge). Gitelson Merzlyak 1994. GNDVI (NIR minus Green) divided (NIR plus Green). Gitelson 1996. CIred NIR divided RedEdge, minus one. Gitelson 2003. MCARI quantity RedEdge minus Red minus 0.2 times quantity RedEdge minus Green, multiplied ratio RedEdge Red. Daughtry 2000. MSAVI2 2 NIR + 1 minus square root (2 NIR + 1)^2 minus 8 times NIR minus Red, divided two. Qi 1994. NDVI saturates LAI > 3–4 closed canopies; mid- late-vegetative stages (GS30+ cereals, V8+ maize) red-edge indices (NDRE, CIred) considerably sensitive canopy nitrogen status (Clarke 2001; Li 2014; Cao 2015). NFert variable-rate functions (variable_rate_N(), estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve(), estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers()) accept normalised VI 0–1 scale, user can substitute NDVI NDRE GNDVI transparently.

Photochem. Photobiol. B 22. Gitelson, .. et al. (1996). Use green channel remote sensing global vegetation EOS-MODIS. Remote Sens. Environ. 58, 289-298. Gitelson, .. et al. (2003). Relationships leaf chlorophyll content spectral reflectance. J. Plant Physiol. 160, 271-282. Daughtry, C.S.T. et al. (2000). Estimating corn leaf chlorophyll concentration leaf canopy reflectance. Remote Sens. Environ. 74. Qi, J. et al. (1994). modified soil adjusted vegetation index. Remote Sens. Environ. 48, 119-126. Clarke, T.R. et al. (2001). Remote sensing nitrogen status wheat. Proc. title: Crop-specific parameters for the Nitrogen Nutrition Index pipeline — crop_params_NNI
text: , b Coefficients critical-N dilution curve \(N_c(\%) = \cdot W^{-b}\), W t DM ha\(^{-1}\). Lemaire & Gastal (1997); Justes et al. (1994) wheat; Plenet & Lemaire (2000) maize; Sheehy et al. (1998) rice; Colnenne et al. (1998) rapeseed. w_min Lower bound validity dilution curve (t DM ha\(^{-1}\)). NNI masked biomass. alpha_leaf Leaf--total-aboveground biomass allocation coefficient vegetative stages NNI meant . W_total = W_leaf / alpha_leaf, typical partitioning 0.30 (maize) / 0.35 (wheat) / 0.40 (rice) rescales GPR-derived leaf biomass aboveground biomass. k_NChl Canopy-level N : chlorophyll ratio used converting CNC_Cab (g Chl m\(^{-2}\) ground) canopy N (g N m\(^{-2}\)). Values 50-80 range; defaults reported Houles et al. (2007) Baret et al. (2007) first-guess Sentinel-2. used pipeline fed CNC_Cprot (protein-retrieval path, already returns canopy N directly). fvc_min Minimum fractional vegetation cover required spectral signal dominated canopy soil. Pixels threshold masked NNI retrieval.

title: Crop-specific parameters for the Nitrogen Nutrition Index pipeline — crop_params_NNI
text: dilution-curve coefficients b well established listed crops across European cropping systems; rarely need recalibration. alpha_leaf k_NChl two locally sensitive parameters. -calibrated values commonly introduce 15-30pct bias final NNI map. minimal calibration dataset consists 30-50 plot-level samples across growing season, analysed dry biomass (oven 65 degC) total plant N (Kjeldahl Dumas), paired matching Sentinel-2 acquisition date. NNI meaningful inside crop-specific phenological windows (e.g. GS31-GS55 wheat, V6-V12 maize). Outside windows critical-N curve valid. scene dominated partially covered pixels (FVC < 0.5), GPR retrievals degrade soil signal enters BRDF. Use fvc_min prune .

title: GPR-based estimation of canopy biophysical variables from Sentinel-2 L2A — estimate_biophysical
text: function pure-R port pyeogpr inference pipeline (Estevez et al., 2022). depends terra (raster /O) jsonlite (model loading) produces numerical output reference Python implementation machine precision (correlation 1.0, relative error < 1.4 \(\cdot 10^{-8}\) 200-pixel benchmark). models trained PROSAIL + canopy N simulations distributed *.json files.

title: GPR-based estimation of canopy biophysical variables from Sentinel-2 L2A — estimate_biophysical
text: S2 raster expected store DN values ESA L2A convention (offset -1000, scale 10000). default function applies rescaling; pass apply_offset = FALSE raster already surface-reflectance units (0-1.5). One GeoTIFF written per requested variable (plus _unc.tif file prediction standard deviation variables whose model exposes pre-computed Linv_pre_calc matrix). Output rasters inherit geometry (CRS, extent, resolution) input.

N_actual (% DM) follows CNC_Cprot (canopy N content, g m\(^{-2}\) ground) divided biomass per unit ground area. helper biophysical_to_NNI_inputs wraps conversion.

title: Export a variable-rate prescription map for farm machinery — export_prescription
text: Writes prescription layer (sf polygons object raster variable_rate_N / farm_balance) one formats accepted -board monitors modern tractors sprayers. Supported formats: plain Shapefile, GeoJSON, KML, GeoPackage, John Deere-ready Shapefile, Trimble- ready Shapefile ISOXML TASKDATA (ISOBUS ISO 11783-10).

title: Export a variable-rate prescription map for farm machinery — export_prescription

title: Export a variable-rate prescription map for farm machinery — export_prescription
text: x Input map. Either sf polygons object (returned farm_balance) RasterLayer / SpatRaster (returned variable_rate_N). Rasters polygonised via terra::.polygons() export. path single-file formats, output path (proper extension: .shp, .geojson, .kml, .gpkg). ISOXML, output directory (TASKDATA.XML file created inside). format Character. One "shp", "geojson", "kml", "gpkg", "johndeere", "trimble", "isoxml". Case-insensitive; NULL, inferred file extension. dose_field Character. Name numeric column carrying N rate (kg N ha\(^{-1}\)). Defaults try "N_target", "dose", "rate" order. area_min Numeric. Drop polygon slivers smaller area square metres (default 1). isoxml_opts Named list metadata used format = "isoxml": task_name, field_name, crop, product, unit, ddi_code. optional, sensible defaults applied.

what: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
text: Alternative GPR-based compute_NNI_from_S2 pipeline lets user derive Nitrogen Nutrition Index map directly single vegetation index raster (NDRE, NDVI, red-edge chlorophyll index) using published crop-specific empirical regression. approach less physically rigorous PROSAIL-trained GPR pipeline require model files runs single band ratio.

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
code: nni_from_vi_empirical(   vi_raster,   index = c("NDRE", "NDVI", "CIred_edge"),   crop = "wheat",   slope = NULL,   intercept = NULL,   nni_range = c(0.5, 1.5),   nni_thresholds = c(0.9, 1.1) )

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
text: vi_raster RasterLayer / SpatRaster normalised vegetation index (dimensionless, typically 0-1). index Character. vegetation index used. One "NDRE", "NDVI", "CIred_edge". crop Character. One "wheat", "maize", "rice", "barley". Case-insensitive; Italian aliases ("frumento", "mais", "orzo") accepted. slope, intercept Optional numeric overrides linear regression NNI = slope * VI + intercept. must supplied together. nni_range Numeric length-2 vector giving clipping range output (default c(0.5, 1.5)). nni_thresholds Numeric length-2 vector lower upper NNI thresholds zone classification (default c(0.90, 1.10)).

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
text: named list two SpatRaster / RasterLayer objects: NNI (continuous) zones (integer 1 / 2 / 3 deficient / optimal / excessive).

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
text: default equations ship lookup table tied index crop. reproduce commonly cited regressions Cao et al. (2013) rice (NDRE), Cilia et al. (2014) Magney et al. (2017) wheat (NDRE), Li et al. (2014) maize (Cired-edge), Fitzgerald et al. (2010) wheat (NDVI). linear vegetation index: $$NNI = \cdot VI + b$$ Users strongly encouraged replace b locally calibrated values (via slope intercept arguments) whenever ground-truth dataset available. NNI clipped user-configurable range (default 0.5-1.5).

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
text: Cao Q, Miao Y, Wang H, Huang S, Cheng S, Khosla R, Jiang R. Non-destructive estimation rice plant nitrogen status Crop Circle multispectral active canopy sensor. Field Crops Res 2013;154:133-144. Fitzgerald G, Rodriguez D, O'Leary G. Measuring predicting canopy nitrogen nutrition wheat using spectral index - canopy chlorophyll content index (CCCI). Field Crops Res 2010;116:318-324.

title: Empirical NNI from a vegetation index (no GPR models required) — nni_from_vi_empirical
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(raster) ndre <- raster("ndre.tif") out  <- nni_from_vi_empirical(ndre, index = "NDRE", crop = "wheat") plot(out$NNI) } # }

title: Calculate soil fertility parameters (B = B1 + B2 in DPI). — soil_fertility
text: Ntot Total nitrogen content soil (e.g. g/kg per mille). SOM Soil organic matter content (%). soil.group Soil group (e.g., "Sandy textures"). CN Carbon--nitrogen ratio. ccp Crop category planting period (e.g., "Autumn-winter crop <150 days"). soil_seeding Seeding mode, one "traditional" (default) "-till". DPI 2026 applies detrazione 3 kg N/ha b1 (readily available N, "N pronto") semina su sodo, tillage-driven mineralisation absent. See foglio B, cella Detrazione per semina su sodo = 3 Fert_Office v1.26. coefN_readily, coefN_mineralised, coef_time Lookup tables.

title: Calculate soil fertility parameters (B = B1 + B2 in DPI). — soil_fertility

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
text: Applies N_balance() pixel--pixel set soil-property rasters, returning one raster layers spatially-resolved balance terms.

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
code: spatial_N_balance(   soil_stack,   expected_yield_tons_ha,   crop,   ccp,   oxygen_availability = "Normal",   winter_rain,   start_spring_rain,   prev_crop,   source = "Cattle slurry",   fertorg_frequency = "every year",   location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",   forg_quantity = 0,   terms = c("A", "B", "C1", "C2", "D", "E", "F", "Forg", "G", "N_to_apply"),   ... )

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
text: soil_stack RasterStack (RasterBrick) whose layers encode soil properties vary space. Required layers (matched name, case-insensitive): TN Total soil nitrogen, pct SOM Soil organic matter, pct Clay Clay content, pct Sand Sand content, pct CNratio Carbon--nitrogen ratio (dimensionless) expected_yield_tons_ha Numeric scalar. Expected crop yield (t ha\(^{-1}\)). crop Character. Crop name (Italian English, see resolve_crop()). ccp Character. Crop calendar period. oxygen_availability Character. Oxygen availability class (default "Normal"). winter_rain, start_spring_rain Numeric scalars. Rainfall (mm). prev_crop Character. Previous crop. source, fertorg_frequency, location, forg_quantity Organic-fertilization parameters (see N_balance()). terms Character vector balance terms return layers output RasterStack. Default c("", "B", "C1", "C2", "D", "E", "F", "Forg", "G", "N_to_apply"). Use "" return every column N_balance() output. ... Additional arguments passed N_balance().

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
text: RasterStack one layer per requested balance term, resolution, extent CRS soil_stack.

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
text: Agronomic climatic parameters depend location within field (yield target, crop, rainfall, previous crop, organic history) treated scalars: spatial variability balance driven exclusively soil-property rasters. function iterates non-NA cells. 96 \(\times\) 101 raster (~10 000 pixels) computation takes approximately one second modern laptop.

title: Spatially-explicit Nitrogen Balance — spatial_N_balance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(raster) ext <- system.file("extdata", package = "NFert") soil <- raster::stack(   file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_TN.tif"),   file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_SOM.tif"),   file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_Clay.tif"),   file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_Sand.tif"),   file.path(ext, "Cremonesi_CNratio.tif") ) names(soil) <- c("TN", "SOM", "Clay", "Sand", "CNratio")  n_map <- spatial_N_balance(   soil_stack = soil,   expected_yield_tons_ha = 60,   crop = "Mais trinciato (classe 700)",   ccp  = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days",   oxygen_availability = "Normal",   winter_rain = 160, start_spring_rain = 40,   prev_crop = "Winter cereals straw removal",   source = "Cattle slurry", fertorg_frequency = "every year",   location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",   forg_quantity = 100 ) raster::plot(n_map[["N_to_apply"]]) } # }

what: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
text: Bridges constant-rate output N_balance() / calculate_N_fertilization() scheda_N() spatial NDVI-based variable-rate estimators NFert (estimate_N_rate_from_calibration_curve() estimate_N_rate_from_holland_schepers()).

title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
code: variable_rate_N(   ndvi_raster,   n_dose,   method = c("calibration", "holland"),   envelope = 0.25,   minN = NULL,   maxN = NULL,   mas_cap = NULL,   plot = FALSE )

title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
text: ndvi_raster raster::RasterLayer NDVI (0–1), RasterBrick / RasterStack (layer NDVI present, otherwise first layer). n_dose Numeric. Field-average N dose (kg/ha) N_balance() + calculate_N_fertilization(), scheda_N()$dose_final, custom target. method Either "calibration" (default, two-point linear) "holland" (Holland & Schepers sufficiency index). envelope Numeric (0, 1). Half-amplitude dose range around n_dose calibration method. Default 0.25 (.e. minN = 0.75 * n_dose, maxN = 1.25 * n_dose). minN, maxN Optional explicit override envelope (kg/ha). mas_cap Numeric NULL. provided, caps per-pixel rate never exceeds MAS limit (e.g. get_MAS()). plot Logical, passed underlying estimator. Default FALSE.

title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
text: list : rate_raster RasterLayer N rate per pixel (kg/ha). mean_kg_ha Mean rate non-NA pixels (kg/ha). min_kg_ha, max_kg_ha Min max rate non-NA pixels. n_dose_input input field-average N dose. method method used.

title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
text: Two integration patterns supported: Calibration curve (method = "calibration"): agronomic balance determines target field-average dose; function derives minN/maxN envelope around (default +/- 25%) applies linear NDVI-driven calibration (low NDVI = N, high NDVI = less N). Holland & Schepers (method = "holland"): agronomic balance used base N rate H&S sufficiency-index algorithm. output preserves field-average dose computed balance: raster variable rates integrates (mean) approximately input n_dose, ensuring agronomic constraint (MAS, ZVN) respected.

title: Variable-rate nitrogen from balance + NDVI — variable_rate_N
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(raster) library(NFert) # NDVI raster data(s2.rast) ndvi <- s2.rast  # 1) Compute agronomic dose with the balance bal <- N_balance(expected_yield_tons_ha = 6,                  crop = "Grano duro (pianta intera)",                  ccp  = "Spring-summer crop 100-130 days",                  sand = 15.5, clay = 18.5,                  Ntot = 1.5, SOM = 2, CN = 7.73,                  oxygen_availability = "Normal",                  winter_rain = 150, start_spring_rain = 0,                  prev_crop = "Maize stalks removed",                  source = "None", fertorg_frequency = "every year",                  location = "Plain adjacent to urbanized areas",                  forg_quantity = 0) n_dose <- calculate_N_fertilization(bal)   # ~142 kg/ha  # 2) Spatialise via NDVI calibration vr <- variable_rate_N(ndvi, n_dose = n_dose, method = "calibration",                       mas_cap = get_MAS("Grano duro (pianta intera)")$N_max) raster::plot(vr$rate_raster) vr$mean_kg_ha } # }
